$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 17 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: On the eve of San Marino the king said it out loud, serene, unhurried, final: of all the crowns he has gathered, this is the only one left worth keeping, and the summit season had its epigraph. The MADONNA 456 ran the chapters like a house that has decided which line history will remember, the PALM 190 V10 immaculate, the benchmark ledger's 69 wins and 72 poles deepening with every round. Asselin took his definitive home telling at France with a butler's grace. The mood in the yellow-and-red garage was unfamiliar: not serenity, exactly. Something nearer appetite.
team.madonna.objective: Keep the only crown worth keeping, and deepen the deepest ledger the almanac holds.
team.madonna.definingEvent: The eve of San Marino: of all the crowns, the king says, this is the only one left worth keeping.
team.madonna.carryForward: Beyond the flag the almanac's last page waits, and the king's ledger is already the deepest it keeps.

team.firenze.summary: The men who built the mountain watched the climb, and the coldest of them brought crampons. Elssler lent the summit its gravity and its threat in the same silence, the FIRENZE 500's FIRENZE 99 V12 singing its last jewel season at full aristocratic pitch. Hungary gave Germi the romance of the twisting walls, Italy staged his temple chapter a week later, and the tifosi rose on schedule. The wine-dark house's campaign was the connoisseur's kind: excellent, honoured, and third in nobody's telling this time. The interruption it has chased for a decade had never looked so near.
team.firenze.objective: Lend the summit its gravity, and give the interruption one last alpine stage.
team.firenze.definingEvent: Hungary's walls and Italy's temple, a week apart: the wine-dark house's double chapter, given in full.
team.firenze.carryForward: The wine-dark jewel enters whatever the almanac opens next with its bloodline intact and its ice unmelted.

team.millions.summary: The gold gave the summit its elder and its volume. Alberti, patrician emeritus, lent the age's final campaign the gravity of a man who opened it, walking the grid like a landlord inspecting what his money built. Jones saved his loudest roar for Great Britain, and the home crowd returned it with interest. The MILLIONS 189's DICK MD V10 sang its definitive jewel specification, polished past argument. The accountants closed the era's ledger without one abandoned memo, which the garage read as growth. What the fortune bought, it turned out, was a seat at the summit's table, occupied loudly.
team.millions.objective: Close the age's account loud, excellent, and seated at the summit's table.
team.millions.definingEvent: Great Britain: Jones's home roar returned with interest, the patrician watching like a landlord.
team.millions.carryForward: The gold's account of the age closes loud and unembarrassed by its total, whatever the total is.

team.bestowal.summary: The forest cathedral read its canon aloud at full speed. At West Germany the tannoy gave Blume's straight-line legend its definitive telling, the Gen-5s holding lap records inside the Charter's rebuilt walls, the Reckoning's promise kept at full throttle, and the BESTOWAL 167's sealed VAPOR DN was the afternoon's whole argument. Picos kept the Sundays warm to the last. The marble house ran the summit with patrician completeness, correct everywhere, threatening at the cathedrals. The benefactor's autumn review contained one sentence the garage had framed: the money was expectation, and the expectation was met.
team.bestowal.objective: Read the forest canon at full speed, and meet expectation at the cathedrals.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: West Germany: the forest-straight canon read aloud, lap records held inside the Charter's walls.
team.bestowal.carryForward: The cathedral's canon is read; the benefactor's next expectation is already being drawn.

team.blanche.summary: The intruder of the horsepower years arrived at the summit as an equal, and knocked on nothing. Herbin's spotless craft had become the standard the aristocrats measure themselves against, the BLANCHE 582's DELTA 103 V10 the grid's standing lecture in thrift and thunder, and the white car ran the chapters like a house that had stopped asking permission. White grinned through his final campaign beside the man he believed in first. At season's end the whitecoats opened the champagne, eleven seasons cold, for the equal the white car had become, and allowed themselves one glass each, logged.
team.blanche.objective: Run the summit as an equal, and open the champagne for what a decade built.
team.blanche.definingEvent: The champagne opened at last, eleven seasons cold, and the one glass logged per whitecoat.
team.blanche.carryForward: The door the white car knocked on for a decade is its own now, and the knocking belongs to someone else.

team.tyrant.summary: Two sermons remained in the blades, and both were preached on schedule. Hamano gave Japan its definitive lesson in home discipline, the TYRANT 548 a study in calm at the summit's boiling points, while Canada granted Gould his last late-braking sermon along the wall of champions, the island roaring like a congregation. The LIZZIE 24 V8 finished everything, as it has since the boot camp opened. Australia's last brawl sorted who reached Monaco still breathing, and the gunmetal car reached it with its knuckles intact. The age's final examination, the crew noted, was graded in dents nobody left on them.
team.tyrant.objective: Preach the two last sermons, and reach Monaco with the knuckles intact.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Canada: the last late-braking sermon along the wall of champions, the island on its feet.
team.tyrant.carryForward: The boot camp keeps the wall, the sermons, and the knuckles; the age's examination is graded.

team.losel.summary: The tortoise law got its definitive telling at Spain, and the professor graded the age one last time. Pacheco closed his cold sums through the summit's compressed midfield, the LOSEL 125's VAPOR DNPQ V8 holding every filed curve to the flag, Dehehe's blue metronome ticking out the era. The data door's model of the Crown of Crowns, built a year early from someone else's evidence, matched the season chapter for chapter, a fact the garage declined to publish. The file was closed, labelled, and shelved. The age, in the only currency Losel trades, balances.
team.losel.objective: Grade the age one last time, and close the file labelled solved.
team.losel.definingEvent: Spain: the tortoise law's definitive telling, the professor grading the age one last time.
team.losel.carryForward: The age's sums all balance; the slide rules shelve the file and keep the receipts.

team.may.summary: The youngest crew of the iron years arrived at the summit as the young guard, and nobody thought to gasp. Turner threw the MAY 555 into the summit's fights with the cheerfulness the midfield has feared for a decade, the LORRY 32 V8 finishing with family-dog loyalty, and the leaf-green car's muggings of aristocrats were entered as routine, the big board's deepest compliment. The crew whistled through all sixteen chapters. Spring's ledger of the age: hope with teeth was a strategy, hope with names was a movement, and hope with finishes, it turns out, is an institution.
team.may.objective: Run the summit as the young guard, and whistle through all sixteen chapters.
team.may.definingEvent: The leaf-green mugging of an aristocrat entered as routine, the big board's deepest compliment.
team.may.carryForward: Spring outlives the summit; the youngest crew of the iron years is the establishment's young guard now.

team.bullets.summary: The last fire spent itself the way the backstreet spends everything: in public, to the final coin. The #17 carried its thousand small names into the summit, Brazil staging the insurgent's homecoming, the age's loudest chapter, the drums out for the wet master one final time, and Belgium's forest stood in the rain where the whole grid still remembers paddling in a backstreet car's spray. The BULLETS 560's customer LIZZIE 24 V8 finished what the fire started. The establishment applauded the man it laughed at seventeen summers ago. The lock-up's ledger closed where it opened: empty, and worth everything.
team.bullets.objective: Spend the last fire to the final coin, in public, at the pilgrimage rounds.
team.bullets.definingEvent: Brazil: the insurgent's homecoming, the names still on the car, the drums out one final time.
team.bullets.carryForward: The fortune is spent to the final coin; the names stay on the car, and the almanac keeps them.

team.dardan.summary: The house that learned to let go standing was cheered by the crowd that once jeered it. At Italy the tifosi gave Bellini's oxblood spear the definitive home chapter, the grandstands that booed the feud rising for the man who ended it, and the DARDAN 700 ran the summit freed of every ghost. Alfven's ice banked its usual quiet arithmetic, the VAPOR DNPQ V8 faultless to the flag. The clan's season was the peace dividend, collected every round. The spear hangs in the garage now, unblooded and unbroken, and the clan points at it like other houses point at trophies.
team.dardan.objective: Collect the peace dividend at every round, and stand tall at the temple.
team.dardan.definingEvent: Italy: the tifosi rise for the man who ended the feud, and the spear is hung unblooded.
team.dardan.carryForward: The clan stands in the almanac as the house that learned to let go standing, and stands taller for it.

team.linden.summary: The oldest number on the grid deepened at the summit, exactly where the watch said he would be. Moreau brought the LINDEN LN198 home through the age's fastest season with unhurried green patience, the LORRY 32 V8 seasoned past all argument, the longest single-team tenure the championship has ever kept extended to its final, untouchable line. The veterans' gravity, the paddock called it, and the green garage supplied the largest share. Asked for a farewell speech, the philosopher offered one sentence: the tree does not attend its own autumn. The watch, everyone noted, is still being set.
team.linden.objective: Deepen the untouchable tenure, and let the watch keep being set.
team.linden.definingEvent: The philosopher's one-sentence autumn: the tree does not attend its own.
team.linden.carryForward: The bond no one can imagine touched remains untouched, and the tree is still growing.

team.minarae.summary: MINARAE COUNTRY, one last time. The North American canyon flew the old #20's home colours, and Miller flung the car through the walls with the street roaring his number, the veteran's definitive telling written in brake dust. Then the forge's present took over: Nono crowded the front of the summit as the seasons always said he would, the MINARAE 594 and the SEGA SG1000 V8 equal to everything, the boy who ate hairpins arrived whole. Miller watched from the pit wall, arms folded, out-measured and unbothered. The forge's fire, the paddock agreed, was the age's alarm clock.
team.minarae.objective: Give the canyon its last roar, and crowd the front of the summit.
team.minarae.definingEvent: MINARAE COUNTRY: the #20 flung through the canyon walls with the street roaring his number.
team.minarae.carryForward: The flame does not go back to sleep, and the forge's alarm clock rings into whatever follows.

team.rigel.summary: The old light was counted among the age's stars, and the museum kept the programme. Cotman banked his finishes like birthdays to the last, Chardin held his doors with undimmed courtesy, and the RIGEL 3000's LORRY 32 V8 sang its bright-century song through the summit's compressed packs. The lifers' season was the restorer's vindication: the car they built, not copied, finished the age. The trophy shelf caught the work-lamps every evening of the run-in. The starlight, the lifers agreed at the last flag, had been a destination all along, and they had the mileage to prove it.
team.rigel.objective: Finish the age in a car the museum built, and earn the star's listing.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The star's listing in the age's programme, kept, framed, and dusted daily.
team.rigel.carryForward: The museum's shelf has a page of the age for itself, dusted daily, believed in completely.

team.comet.summary: The romantics brought the faith to the summit and pointed the black car at the sky. Tornio, silent to the end, drove the COMET 323 through the age's final chapters, the LIZZIE 24 V8 finishing with romantics' regularity. Two grey afternoons had been circled since winter; on one of them the matte-black car burned at the old brightness, briefly, and the pit lane looked up on schedule. The pan stayed cold. The faith did not. At the last flag the old men rolled the car inside, and no one said whether it was for the last time.
team.comet.objective: Circle two grey afternoons, and make the pit lane look up one last time.
team.comet.definingEvent: One circled afternoon at the old brightness, and the pit lane looking up on schedule.
team.comet.carryForward: The car rolled inside after the last flag, the pan cold, the faith impossible to kill.

team.orchis.summary: The survivor planted its doctrine in the age's final garden. Tegner read the summit's skies with a professor's calm, the ORCHIS 056 banking its small cool harvests while the loud houses spent themselves on chapters, and the MISFIRE 50 V8 completed its seventh clean season, the wilting years now read aloud as scripture. The gardener-principal's ledger closed the era with the steadiest little campaign under glass. The frost gambler's final note of the age: a flower that survives everything has, by definition, a future. The greenhouse checked the sky one last time, and planted.
team.orchis.objective: Sit the doctrine's final examination, and plant for whatever sky follows.
team.orchis.definingEvent: The MISFIRE 50 V8's seventh clean season, the wilting years read aloud as scripture.
team.orchis.carryForward: The flower outlasted the age; the greenhouse plants whatever sky comes next.

team.zeroforce.summary: One room remained in the age, and the reservation was still good. Klinger drove the ZEROFORCE 231 through the summit with its famous patience, the LORRY 32 V8 finishing the era as routine, and twenty-three garages kept a glass within reach at every round. The chalk wall recorded the floor's finest arithmetic of the age, the #32 a fixture where miracles used to be required. The toast's status after the final flag is a matter the crew declines to discuss, which everyone understood perfectly. The end of the road, it turns out, was an address with a future.
team.zeroforce.objective: Be a fixture at the summit, and keep the glasses within reach at all sixteen rounds.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: The crew declines to discuss the toast after the final flag, and everyone understands.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: The chalk wall keeps its reservation beyond the flag; the floor endures, as the floor does.

team.joke.summary: The un-buried jester spent the summit reminding the circus it had tried to say goodbye. Dufay ran the encore that will not end, sixteen rounds sold out again to boo the man who un-retired, the JOKE 777's POND V8 running thriftily beneath the theatre. Monaco echoed every lap: the tunnel where he un-announced it, the hairpin he swears he greased, the grid where the laugh landed. The ledger held in LEVEL B. At the last flag the carnival took its bow with a straight face, and declined to confirm whether the farewell tour would have a sequel.
team.joke.objective: Run the encore that will not end, and bow at the last flag with a straight face.
team.joke.definingEvent: Sixteen rounds of the encore that will not end, sold out to boo the un-retirement.
team.joke.carryForward: The punchline outlived its own farewell, and the crown jewel's loudest laugh is his forever.

team.lares.summary: The hearth volunteered, again, to be the garage that never changes. Arai brought the LARES 92 home through the summit with courteous reliability, the RAM V12 finishing the age as it finished every age: entire. The mantel filled at the rate the house calls abundance, the kettle never went off the boil, and the old hands filled the doorway at every round of the finale. The lamp stayed lit. When the last flag fell, the hearth-red car was still circulating, which the paddock agreed was the correct order of things. Supper, the house confirmed, was at seven.
team.lares.objective: Keep the lamp lit through the finale, and be circulating when it falls.
team.lares.definingEvent: The last flag falls with the hearth-red car still circulating, the correct order of things.
team.lares.carryForward: The lamp stays lit past the last flag; the hearth outlasts the age, as planned.

team.feet.summary: The grafters brought the invoice to the summit and punched the clock. Rampal dragged the FEET 13 through the age's final chapters with all three tools earning, the YOUGEN V10 answering every ask, and Old Boots came home in the points the garage calls wages while grandees' machinery fed the treeline around it. The pit lane's caps went one notch lower at every round, which at Feet is a title. The ledger of the age balanced in sweat: everything earned, nothing given, and the hands that built part of the jewel era finally photographed at the summit, calluses and all.
team.feet.objective: Invoice the summit in sweat, and balance the age's ledger.
team.feet.definingEvent: The callused hands photographed at the summit: the men who built part of the jewel era.
team.feet.carryForward: The ledger of the age balances in sweat, and the grafters' hands built part of it.

team.serga.summary: The author of the parable climbed the summit with greasy hands. Sambena welded, drove, and finished, the SERGA 1000's SC3000 F12 stubborn to the last lap of the age, the thousand small names on the flanks through all sixteen chapters. The mountain man heard his creed quoted in the summit's speeches and went back to the welding. The grey car finished the fastest season ever timed, built by one pair of hands, paid for by whip-round, answered to no boardroom. At the final flag the pit lane found him under the car. The thanks, he noted, were already on it.
team.serga.objective: Weld, drive, finish the age, and be found under the car at the flag.
team.serga.definingEvent: The grey car finishes the fastest season ever timed: one pair of hands, no boardroom.
team.serga.carryForward: The welding continues beyond the flag; the thanks were already on the car.

team.cool.summary: The deckchairs faced the greatest show of the age and did not move an inch. Delvaux chased the light through the summit's chapters, the COOL 05's CORSE V8 humming its sunset tune, and at Belgium the garage kept the chairs out in the forest rain one last time, by choice, for the view. The festivals came at their intervals and were celebrated until the light failed. When the tunnel went dark at Monaco, the glass was raised to the whole age. The big board said what it liked for seventeen seasons. The light, every single evening, went gold.
team.cool.objective: Watch the age's greatest show from the same seats, and raise the glass at the tunnel.
team.cool.definingEvent: Belgium: the deckchairs kept out in the forest rain one last time, by choice, for the view.
team.cool.carryForward: The last flag falls, the light goes gold, and the glass is raised to the whole age.

team.moon.summary: The orbit reached the summit's sky, and the telescope finally had company. Yepes waved the leaders through one last season, grinning from closer than ever, the MOON 292's RAM V12 completing the age's full orbit of finishes, and the last flag fell with the silver car present, circled in chalk. The low voices' final measurement of the era: the gap that was once a distance is now, at worst, a margin. Every moon is on the rise, the garage said at season's end, and this one had risen. The telescope stays up. The sky, it turns out, was always reachable.
team.moon.objective: Complete the orbit's full account of finishes, and be present at the last flag.
team.moon.definingEvent: The last flag circled in chalk: the silver car present, the orbit complete.
team.moon.carryForward: Every moon rises; the telescope turns, patiently, to whatever sky follows the age.

team.iris.summary: The vow met its deadline with the clock deafening. Portugal staged the slingshot legend's definitive telling, the home crowd roaring the promise into its final autumn, and Salgado burned through the summit with the IRIS 717 at the razor's edge, the PRISM 90 V10 singing at its oldest pitch. Australia, his second home, sorted the last brawl before the crown with the fire in the thick of it. Across the wall the frost ran at her peak, and the white gloves did not look. The campaign was total, elegant, and loud. The fire, to the final flag, had not cooled.
team.iris.objective: Race the vow to its deadline, and burn to the final flag.
team.iris.definingEvent: Portugal: the slingshot legend's definitive telling, the vow roared into its final autumn.
team.iris.carryForward: The vow met its deadline under the last flag, and the whole circus stayed for the answer.

team.azalea.summary: The frost arrived at her absolute peak, and Mexico waited like a cathedral. Larssen's cold arithmetic made the AZALEA 808 the summit's coldest argument, the BLOOM 88 V8 flawless, the rooftop lung breathing thin air where the field gasped, and the glasshouse's decade of cultivation stood at full flower on the big board. The sister house's fire burned loud across the wall and was treated as weather. The bloom, once open, never closed. Whatever the last flag read, the frost's season was the one the copybooks will keep: patience, spent all at once, at the summit it was planted for.
team.azalea.objective: Spend a decade's patience at the summit, and keep the cold hinge at Mexico.
team.azalea.definingEvent: Mexico: the rooftop lung breathing thin air while the field gasps, the cold hinge on schedule.
team.azalea.carryForward: The decade's cultivation is spent at the summit; what the frost wintered into, the almanac keeps.
